author: @HaleRegales
text: What a brilliant project - don't listen to snippy comments from elitists with no vision, it's a great idea and a totally worthy experiment, even if it doesn't pan out we learn from fiddling with things.  Thanks Robert - I love your videos keep up the excellent and inspiring work.

author: @dustymiller1813
text: Brilliant idea Robert. Just a quick idea, when/if you varnish the top, adding a small amount of kiln-dried sand at the same time, will increase foot-grip dramatically.

author: @PVflying
text: So pleased to see you banging the drum for gorilla glue. It’s a well kept secret but once discovered, it unlocks so many possibilities!  Not to be confused with the Gorilla glue branded cyanoacrylate or the white gorilla glue. The original brown one is best by far 👍👍👌

replies.author: @ArbitraryOnslaught
replies.text:
I love that glue!!! Until you get it on your hands and try to wash them 😝

Oh ps: if you have a "mister" you can spray the glue with water to have it "activate" "cure" glue better.

author: @Gainn
text:
It's still a bit too high, but if you're going to keep the height you need to upgrade the bushings on the trucks to harder ones (98 or maybe even 100A). 
More like those you'd use for ramp skating. When you start picking up some speed with that height over pivot point it's going to be a nightmare to ride with spongy trucks, and even worse if you power it. (they can get power wobbles even with harder bushings using narrow trucks).

author: @ChristieNel
text: How did you get the screw holes in the same place for the three sheets?

replies.author: @Rem_NL
replies.text: i dont think you need them to be in the same place, since the glue is what holds it down, just make sure you remove the first screws when the glue is dry and you attach the next board :)

replies.author: @colleenforrest7936
replies.text: You could use a sheet of paper with a screw hole template on it. Get you close anyway.
